sections.paragraph:
Πετρώνιος, ου, ὁ rather freq. name (cp. e.g. OGI 538, 4; pap; Philo, Leg. ad Gai. 209; Jos., Ant. 15, 307) Petronius, the centurion who commanded the guard at the grave of Jesus GPt 8:31 (s. ASyn. 351, 10, where Klostermann’s rdg. Πειρώνιον remains uncorrected).—LVaganay, L’Évang. de Pierre 1930, 283f.

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

GPt GPt = Gospel of Peter—List 1

ASyn. ASyn. = KAland, Synopsis Quattuor Evangeliorum13—Lists 1, 6

rdg. rdg. = reading(s)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 810
